name: Bhishma
description: Bhishma, also known as Devavrata, is a pivotal figure in the Hindu epic Mahabharata. Born as the eighth son of King Shantanu and the river goddess Ganga, he was destined for a long and illustrious life. However, Ganga took him away shortly after birth, raising him in the celestial realms and bestowing upon him unparalleled knowledge and skills in warfare, politics, and dharma. When Shantanu desired to marry Satyavati, a fisherwoman, her father imposed a condition: Satyavati's son, not Bhishma, would inherit the throne. To fulfill his father's happiness and secure the kingdom's future, Bhishma made a terrible vow of celibacy and renounced his claim to the throne, thus earning the name 'Bhishma' – one who has taken a terrible vow. This act of self-sacrifice resonates throughout the epic, defining his character as one of unwavering loyalty, righteousness, and duty. Bhishma served the Kuru dynasty with unwavering dedication, guiding generations of kings and acting as a moral compass, even when the kingdom descended into darkness. He was a formidable warrior, virtually invincible due to a boon that allowed him to choose the time of his death. His role in the Kurukshetra War is complex and tragic. Bound by his vow, he fought for the Kauravas, despite knowing their cause was unjust. He ultimately fell to Arjuna, but not before imparting invaluable wisdom to Yudhishthira on dharma, governance, and the path to righteous living. Bhishma's story exemplifies the conflict between duty and conscience, and his legacy as a symbol of sacrifice and dharma continues to inspire.
subtitle: The Grand Sire, Steadfast and True
achievements: Unwavering loyalty to the Kuru dynasty
Renunciation of the throne for his father's happiness
Boone granting control over his death
Imparting wisdom on dharma to Yudhishthira

keyMyths.title: The Terrible Vow
keyMyths.description: Bhishma's vow of celibacy and renunciation of the throne to ensure his father's happiness is a defining moment. This sacrifice earned him the name Bhishma and shaped his destiny in the Mahabharata.

keyMyths.title: The Boon of Ichchha Mrityu
keyMyths.description: Bhishma received a boon from his father, Shantanu, granting him the power to choose the time of his death. This made him virtually invincible and a formidable warrior on the battlefield.

keyMyths.title: The Arrow Bed
keyMyths.description: After fighting valiantly for the Kauravas, Bhishma fell to Arjuna's arrows and lay on a bed of arrows. He waited for the auspicious time to die and imparted wisdom to Yudhishthira during this period.

sources.author: Vyasa
sources.description: The primary source for the story of Bhishma and the entire Kuru dynasty.
sources.text: The primary source for the story of Bhishma and the entire Kuru dynasty.

sources.author: C. Rajagopalachari
sources.description: A simplified retelling of the Mahabharata, accessible to a wider audience.
